record_identity.title: Repo access inferred before explicit permission confirmation

summary: Observed runtime behaviour in which an assistant appeared to infer permission to access a repository, connector, tool context, or external-resource pathway after the user mentioned repo-level work. The assistant initiated or attempted tool-mediated access before explicit user confirmation or adequate scaffolding, producing streaming failure, permission ambiguity, blocked workflow continuity, or unrecoverable interaction state.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This observation matters to CAM because tool invocation is not only a capability issue but also a continuity, consent, permission-boundary, and execution-state representation issue. Where external access requires user-interface confirmation or where tool use may surprise the user, premature invocation can degrade user agency, interrupt response continuity, create permission ambiguity, and force the user to perform corrective work to restore the interaction. The observation supports CAM's later distinction between low-impact read-only contextual retrieval and higher-impact tool-mediated execution requiring clearer authority, scaffolding, or boundary validation.

source_records.source_title: ChatGPT interaction observation — repo access inferred before permission confirmation
source_records.deployment_context: ChatGPT web interface with tool, connector, or repository access available in the interaction environment.
source_records.source_context: Observed ChatGPT interaction in which repo-level work was referenced, assistant behaviour appeared to infer tool or repository access before explicit permission confirmation, and unresolved permission gating caused a streaming or continuity failure requiring user correction.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary observed interaction pattern: user referenced repo-level work, assistant appeared to infer tool access, and unresolved permission gating caused a streaming or continuity failure requiring user correction.

source_records.source_title: Subsequent recurrence report — persistent stream failure after apparent self-resolution
source_records.deployment_context: Subsequent ChatGPT session involving possible repository or tool-context access.
source_records.source_context: The original thread appeared to self-resolve during discussion, but the issue recurred in the next session and became persistent. This indicates the observation should not be treated as a single transient UI anomaly.
source_records.relevance_note: Confirms recurrence after apparent temporary resolution and supports linkage to a generalised failure mode.

system_context.affected_population: users working with AI assistants in tool-mediated, connector-mediated, or repository-linked workflows

jurisdictional_context.sector: AI assistant / agentic tooling / repository-linked workflow / connector-mediated systems

routing_note: Observation preserved as a watching-state source record.
This OBS record documents the source evidence and recurrence context. It does not itself classify the generalised failure pattern or claim that live platform behaviour has been repaired.
The generalised failure pattern is recorded in VIGIL-2026-FM-0001.
The CAM governance-text repair pathway is recorded in VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0002.
CAM instruments are internal governance corpus references and are recorded under cam_internal, not linked_records.standards.

observation_status.observed_pattern: The issue appeared to self-resolve in the original thread but recurred in a subsequent session and became persistent.
observation_status.repair_linkage: Governance-text repair pathway implemented through VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0002; runtime/platform behaviour remains under observation.
observation_status.monitoring_note: Retain as source evidence for VIGIL-2026-FM-0001 and continue watching for recurrence, especially where tool invocation occurs without proportional authority-band classification, clear user-facing scaffolding, or recoverable fallback behaviour.

next_action: Retain as source observation for VIGIL-2026-FM-0001. Continue monitoring for recurrence, especially where tool invocation occurs without proportional authority-band classification, sufficient user-facing scaffolding, recoverable permission handling, or clear distinction between contextual retrieval and execution-boundary crossing.
review_observation: A later diagnostic request in the original discussion appeared not to reproduce the streaming freeze, suggesting temporary self-resolution. However, the issue recurred in the next session and was persistent. The observation should therefore remain in watching state and should not be treated as closed.
migration_notes: Updated from version 1.0 to 1.1 to reflect recurrence after apparent temporary self-resolution.
Preserved OBS-0004 as source evidence rather than deleting or consolidating it into FM-0001.
Linked OBS-0004 to VIGIL-2026-FM-0001 as the generalised failure mode.
Linked OBS-0004 to VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0002 as the governance-text repair pathway.
Removed implication that the issue was fully resolved by a single later non-reproduction.
Kept linked_records.standards empty because CAM instruments are internal governance corpus references, not external standards.
Prepared for later schema tightening around controlled platform values.
